purpose: The commit adds a check to prevent queuing commands after HCI_UNREGISTER, avoiding a use-after-free bug.
Removes an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h in n_hdlc.c to clean up header dependencies.
The commit updates documentation to reflect changes in store metric 'N/A', improving clarity.
Fixes race conditions and incorrect lock handling during vma split and unmapping by properly allocating new locks for new vm_areas and updating sharing conditions.
Adds AVDD and MICVDD supply documentation to device tree bindings for Realtek rt5682s codec.
The commit fixes a resource leak by adding an iounmap() call in the remove function of the zorro7xx driver.
Removes an unused 'work' field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx to clean up the code.
Changed struct member key_idx from unsigned to signed to allow -1 and prevent static checker warnings.
The commit sorts and removes commented-out CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in RISC-V defconfigs to align with savedefconfig, with no functional impact.
Refactors code by replacing open-coded checks with a helper function to improve maintainability and readability.
Modifies self-tests to skip RTC tests when hardware isn't present, improving test reliability.
Removes redundant assignment of variable 'bytes' that was causing a dead store warning.
Decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks to reduce unnecessary code inclusion and improve efficiency.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase 'be split' in a comment within spte.h, improving code readability.
Reverts a change in clock table population for dcn315 to fix an underflow issue caused by using dcfclk-based tables when states are disabled.
Adds compatible string for Renesas RZ/V2L SoC's Mali-G31 GPU in device tree bindings.
The commit renames the struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp across multiple files to improve naming consistency within the codebase.

Fixes a clang -Wformat warning by correcting format string from `%#06hx` to `%#06x` to properly handle integer promotion of `u16` to `int`, improving code correctness and preventing potential future bugs.
Fixes a bug in branch range check by casting to long for correct signed comparison, addressing a warning.
Removes HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD handling by directly calling rtl8188e_set_p2p_ps_offload_cmd() instead of using SetHwReg8188EU(). This simplifies code and reduces the HAL layer.
The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in the arm64 CPU errata code.
Removes incorrect interrupt flags from device tree bindings for MDSS nodes, correcting configuration.
Refactors dynamic array handling to a more general approach, renaming variables and functions to support array size changes from drivers.
Adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs by updating the socinfo driver with new device IDs.
The commit updates the handling of WiFi chip cuts to recognize all possible versions (A-P) instead of labeling some as unknown.
Removes custom enable state tracking for PRCMU clocks, relying on core's count instead.
The commit refactors the code to reduce #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage by grouping related cases together, improving code organization and maintainability.
The commit enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware in the device tree to support future I2C functionality.
This commit improves code comments and fixes minor typos and coding style issues in the thermal driver.
The commit fixes an infinite loop bug in the release_swfw_sync function by adding error handling when failing to acquire hardware semaphore.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI terminology by renaming variables and updating related code, improving code readability.
Removes dead code related to IS_SWAPFILE which was always defined.
Implements GTP echo response functionality to handle Echo Request messages according to specifications.
Replaces cpumask_next with cpumask_first where appropriate and adds cpumask_first_zero function.
Removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR that caused console errors during STA roaming, as firmware no longer supports it.
Adds support for a platform using alc5682 codec without speaker amplifier by adding board configuration and ACPI match entries.
Adds a check to fail zero-copy sendmsg for unsupported sockets, enhancing robustness.
The commit modifies LaTeX documentation to conditionally load xeCJK.sty only if both the font and sty file are present, preventing errors on systems where dependencies might be missing.
The commit modifies the checkpatch script to ignore macros starting with 'case', preventing false error reports.
Refactor simpledrm to use atomic helpers instead of simple-KMS helpers. This improves code architecture and maintainability without changing functionality.
Adds JPEG decode worker interface for parallel HW processing on MT8195.
Removes VPRINTK() calls and ata_scsi_dump_cdb(), replacing them with tracepoints for better logging.
Adds support for entering low-power zstates during extended vblank periods in Freesync mode, improving power efficiency.
The commit fixes a type mismatch causing a CFI failure by correcting the function parameter from uint32_t to enum kfd_preempt_type.
Corrects 'resets' property name in device tree binding and adds validation to prevent invalid configurations.
The commit silences an array-bounds warning in GCC 12 by adding a compiler flag to disable the warning, without addressing the underlying code causing the issue.
Replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify code.
Refactors file installation/removal functions, moving them to appropriate files and adjusting parameters for better code organization.
The commit splits G1 and G2 decoder blocks into separate nodes in device tree bindings for better independence and resource management.
Removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro and integrates its contents into IWL_DEVICE_BZ, reducing code duplication.
